user.title: UniFi Protect Sensors
user.description: Connect to your UniFi console's local Integration API. Generate an API key in the UniFi UI under Settings, Control Plane, Integrations (or in Protect under Settings, API).

data_description.host: Base URL of your console, including the scheme. Most consoles use https on the default port.
data_description.verify_ssl: Most consoles use a self signed certificate. Leave off unless you have installed a trusted one.

reauth_confirm.title: Update API key
reauth_confirm.description: The UniFi console rejected the saved API key. Enter a new Integration API key.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the console. Check the URL and that the console is reachable.
error.invalid_auth: The console rejected the API key.

data.local_enabled: Local account for unsupported accessories (optional, beta)

data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll the console when the websocket push stream is unavailable. The integration always prefers the websocket.
data_description.local_enabled: Optional and not required for normal use. Enable only to read accessories the Integration API key cannot access, currently the UP Air Quality sensor (CO2, VOC, PM, and so on), via the console's internal API. Requires a local UniFi OS account without 2FA. This API is undocumented and may break on Protect firmware updates.
data_description.local_username: Username of a local UniFi OS account (a read-only Viewer role is enough).
data_description.local_password: Password for the local account. Stored in Home Assistant and only sent to your console.

error.invalid_local_auth: The local account credentials were rejected. Check the username and password, and that the account has no 2FA.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the console's local API.
error.local_credentials_required: Enter a username and password, or turn off the air-quality (beta) option.
